description: Robustness of the Study-8 picture-decisiveness verdict across plot resolution (figure 256/512/800 px wide) and the visibility threshold (MIN_PX 2/3/5) -- a 9-setting grid per problem.
motivation: e-0014 established 8/8 picture-decisive at one default plot size + MIN_PX=3. A skeptic can object the verdict is an artifact of those two free parameters. Stress-test it. CPU-only, no agent.
formal_results: For each problem, recompute the picture-decisive predicate over the 3x3 grid of {fig width 3.2,6.4,10.0 in @100dpi -> 256/512/800 px usable} x {MIN_PX 2,3,5}. Report decisive_all_settings and the failing cells.
results_description: 7/8 problems decisive in ALL 9 settings (geom_01/02/03/04/05/09/10). Only geom_06 fails, at the two small-figure cells 256px@MIN3 and 256px@MIN5 (the narrow dip drops below the px threshold on a small plot); decisive at 512/800 px. geom_06 is the corpus's marginal problem on BOTH axes: highest fragility N_all=86 and the sole decisiveness dropout.
conclusions: The picture-decisiveness construction is robust to the two free plot parameters: 7/8 problems are unconditionally decisive across a 4x range of plot widths and 2.5x range of visibility thresholds. geom_06 is the single sensitivity point -- it must be rendered at >= default (512 px) size to stay decisive; a thumbnail plot would miss it. Documented design constraint, not a corpus defect.
